channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Community questions prison release and housing conditions after alleged child murder in Northern Territory
item.description: Community leaders and residents in the Northern Territory are expressing concern and seeking answers regarding the alleged murder of a child by Jefferson Lewis, who had recently been released from pri...

item.title: Blue Jays score eight runs in eighth inning to defeat Twins 11-4
item.description: In a thrilling Major League Baseball matchup, the Toronto Blue Jays overcame the Minnesota Twins with a decisive eight-run surge in the eighth inning, ultimately winning the game 11-4. The Blue Jays’ ...

item.title: Bauchi Governor Bala Mohammed defects from PDP to APM after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: The Governor of Bauchi State, Bala Mohammed, don officially leave the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) and join the Allied Peoples Movement (APM). This move follow Supreme Court ruling wey nullify the p...

item.title: Insights on Crafting a James Bond Theme from an Experienced Composer
item.description: The article, though inaccessible in full, appears to focus on advice and insights from a seasoned composer on how to write a song for the iconic James Bond film franchise. It likely explores the essen...

item.title: Canadiens and Lightning Head to Game 7 After Thrilling Series
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens and Tampa Bay Lightning are heading to a decisive Game 7 in their first-round playoff series after six intense games. The series has been marked by razor-thin margins, with four...

item.title: Trump's Strategy Against Iran Faces Challenges Amid Evolving Warfare and AI Threats
item.description: US President Donald Trump has been using poker metaphors to describe his dealings with foreign powers, claiming that other nations have no cards while he holds all the leverage. However, experts argue...

item.title: Joshua Garcia shares positive experience working with co-stars on “Love Is Never Gone”
item.description: Joshua Garcia expressed his appreciation for the cast of the latest ABS-CBN series “Love Is Never Gone.” He specifically mentioned that working with JMFyang was unexpectedly delightful and that the co...

item.title: Malema criticises anti-immigrant protests, says they fail to address unemployment and inequality
item.description: EFF leader Julius Malema has criticised the recent wave of anti-illegal migrant protests led by the March and March Movement, arguing that they are misdirected and fail to address South Africa’s deepe...

item.title: Wes Streeting Gathers Support for Potential Leadership Challenge Against Keir Starmer
item.description: Wes Streeting, the UK’s Health Secretary, has garnered enough support from Labour MPs to mount a challenge for the leadership of the Labour Party. According to The Telegraph, Streeting now has over 81...

item.title: IMF warns Angola’s debt may reach legal limit; advises using oil windfalls to strengthen finances
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has projected that Angola’s public debt is likely to hit the ceiling set under its fiscal sustainability law in the medium-term. This projection comes amid declin...

item.title: Toronto police investigate potential hate-motivated assault with Orbeez gun
item.description: The Toronto Police Service is seeking the public's help in identifying a vehicle linked to a suspected hate-motivated assault. The incident took place on April 30 in the Bathurst Street and Lawrence A...

item.title: Late-Night Confrontation Between Married Woman and Intruder in Nairobi Caught on Video
item.description: A dramatic confrontation in Nairobi has gone viral after a married woman found another woman inside her home late at night. The video, widely shared on TikTok, shows the furious wife wielding a broom ...

item.title: Nato reviews US plan to redeploy 5,000 troops from Germany
item.description: Nato is seeking clarity on the US decision to pull 5,000 troops out of Germany, a move announced by Donald Trump amid tensions with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz. The German government described th...

item.title: A Tragic Cycle: The Life and Death of Faith Rich and the Failures of the Child Protection System
item.description: Faith Rich's life tragically highlights the deep flaws within the child protection system. From infancy, when she was removed from her mother's care, to her death at 22, Faith experienced instability ...

item.title: Government Cuts Funding to Public Universities by Sh13 Billion Over Three Years
item.description: Over the last three years, Kenyan public universities have faced significant reductions in government funding, according to data from the Kenya National Bureau of Statistics (KNBS). Between the 2023/2...

item.title: Mthatha Residents Protest Over Persistent Water Shortages
item.description: Residents of Esibangweni in Mthatha, Eastern Cape, staged protests on the N2 highway between Mthatha and Qumbu, demanding a reliable water supply. The community, falling under ward 10, last had regula...

item.title: RCAF Major Admits to Illegally Importing Firearms During Return to Canada
item.description: Maj. Kendrick Barling, a Royal Canadian Air Force officer and award-winning marksman, has pleaded guilty to illegally importing eight firearms into Canada. Barling, who previously won the Queen's Meda...

item.title: Three Family Members Die in Muzaffarnagar After Tractor Collides With Motorcycle
item.description: In a tragic incident in Muzaffarnagar, Uttar Pradesh, three members of a family lost their lives when a speeding tractor collided with their motorcycle. The accident occurred on a busy road, causing s...

item.title: Dog Abuse Case Sparked by TikTok Video Goes to Trial
item.description: A viral TikTok video has led to a high-profile animal cruelty case that will soon be in court. Elliot Tshigo, a former owner of a rescued dog named Bobby, faces charges of animal cruelty after social ...

item.title: Families Call for Government Support After Australians Detained in International Waters by Israel
item.description: Six Australians have been detained by the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) after their vessels were intercepted near Crete while participating in the Global Sumud Flotilla, a humanitarian mission carrying ...
